parsed_review.summary: CP00-270 is a descriptor-only RP07 search-core pack (risk A, 10 units, RP07.P09.M05.S19-RP07.P09.M06.S06) closing the M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail (documentation update, command rerun, review receipt placeholder, future correction slot) and opening the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (six review-question rows). Read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms full internal consistency and intact boundaries. (1) Binding integrity: SEARCH_CORE_CP270_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:631) matches the manifest, baseline, closeout-pack-plan (deviation_from_plan:false), and the contract current_pack/review_binding_tail_descriptor/historical_pack_bindings tail (upstream CP00-269, next CP00-271, next_subphase RP07.P09.M06.S07). (2) Distributions: deliverable {implementation:3,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:2,security_audit:2,test:1,ui:1} and micro-title {Permission And Audit Binding:4,Synthetic Fixture Set:6} match SEARCH_CORE_CP270_REQUIREMENTS and the 10 included units. (3) No-write/no-real-data: freezeCp270Result (service.js:4702) stamps ~40 runtime/write flags false; every case-set row is descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false; permission_bypass_detected and leak_detected false; the 1.6MB contract projection mirrors these. (4) Authority boundary: authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true; attestation promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; Claude packet read_only:true with tools [Read,Grep,Glob]. Claude remains a read-only reviewer, not final approver. (5) Hermes boundary: the Hermes packet is descriptor/evidence-only (receipt_shape descriptor_only_..._no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), i.e. deterministic control-plane review/receipt handling, not product runtime. (6) production_ready claim: manifest production_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review; production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification label, not a runtime claim, so there is no overclaim. (7) Review hardening (CP178+): forbidden_review_evidence enumerates auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing refs, and final-approval/trust claims; readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1456-byte valid JSON); validation-report shows prompt 2211B under the 25600B limit, no forbidden tools, and required read-tools inspection. (8) Evidence: command-evidence records all expected gates at exit 0 (node --check/--test, rp05/06/07 validate, plan/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan validate, npm test, npm run validate, build, git diff --check), and the rp07 validator deep-equals contract against registry constants while asserting every boundary. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expectedly absent because they are produced in later hardening steps after this read-only run. The SEARCH_CORE_CP268_ROW_EXTRAS reuse in the CP270 builder follows the established cumulative convention (CP269 reuses it as well; new row keys are absent from the map and guarded by ?? {}) and is benign. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; recommend PASS. Human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim and for opening runtime in a downstream pack.
